one_liner: RKLB surges +16% to $98 on the Iridium acquisition ($8B deal) — transformational news but the stock is still -20% off its June high and the deal math needs scrutiny before chasing.

price_action.intraday_story: RKLB gapped from Friday's $84.54 close to open at $91.29, surged to a high of $99.04 before 11am, pulled back to $89.85 (nearly filling the gap), then rallied to close at $98.01. The intraday dip to $89.85 — a full rejection of the gap fill — is actually constructive: buyers stepped in aggressively at the gap support level. Closing at $98 vs the $99.04 high shows slight resistance just below the $100 round number, which will be the first psychological test in tomorrow's session.

news_catalyst.headline: Rocket Lab to Acquire Iridium Communications for ~$8B in Cash-and-Stock Deal
news_catalyst.summary: Rocket Lab announced it would acquire satellite communications company Iridium at $54/share in a cash-and-stock transaction valued at approximately $8 billion. The deal creates a vertically integrated space company capable of building satellites, launching them on Electron, and operating the network — a major strategic shift from launch-only revenue. KeyBanc upgraded RKLB to Overweight with a $135 price target, calling the company the clear #2 to SpaceX. Context: RKLB had fallen 40% from its June high near $122 into the low $80s before this announcement, so today's +16% recovers only a portion of that drawdown. The Iridium deal also extends a NASA manifest of three dedicated Electron launches starting 2027, plus a record VICTUS HAZE mission for the U.S. Space Force (16h 42min from notice to launch).

technical.summary:
RKLB's RSI at 43.8 is neutral-to-slightly-bearish, not overbought despite the 16% gap — the RSI doesn't fully capture gap moves. The stock remains below both the 50-day EMA ($111.72) and 200-day EMA ($119.20), meaning today's move, while powerful, doesn't yet reverse the intermediate-term downtrend. MACD at -1.65 is still in bearish territory.

The Wyckoff algorithm tags this as Accumulation Phase B with a bullish bias — consistent with the multi-week base building in the $80–$86 range before today's breakout. If the Iridium deal holds investor confidence, the $89.85 intraday low becomes the new line in the sand. A pullback and hold above $90 with drying volume would be the classic Wyckoff LPS (Last Point of Support) entry setup.

The distance to the EMA50 ($111.72) is significant — a 14% gap overhead. RKLB needs to decisively reclaim $99–$100 in the next few sessions and then build a new base before challenging the prior downtrend. The deal size ($8B) vs RKLB's market cap (~$20B at current prices) creates meaningful dilution risk that the market will scrutinize.

wyckoff_narrative:
The Wyckoff algorithm tags RKLB as Accumulation Phase B with bullish bias, which aligns with the multi-week consolidation at $80–$87 after the 40% drawdown from the June peak at $122. Phase B accumulation is characterized by choppy, range-bound trading as institutional participants establish positions — the low-volume retests on June 23–24 were consistent with this phase.

Today's breakout on news could be the SOS (Sign of Strength) that Wyckoff analysts look for at the end of Phase B / beginning of Phase C-D. However, the news-driven nature of the move (M&A announcement) means it's harder to read as organic accumulation. The critical test will be: does RKLB hold above $89–$90 on pullbacks with declining volume? If yes, that's the LPS and Phase C setup. If it fails back below $85, the Iridium deal has been rejected by the market and the accumulation thesis is invalidated.

valuation.summary: DCF is not feasible for RKLB as a standalone entity at this stage. The Iridium acquisition fundamentally changes the financial profile — Iridium generates ~$700M in annual revenue and is cash-flow positive, which would be the first material revenue contribution to RKLB's consolidated financials. Post-deal valuation requires reassessment.

key_strengths: Iridium acquisition creates a vertically integrated launch-and-operate business model
10 consecutive Synspective launches booked through decade-end — growing recurring revenue
VICTUS HAZE record (16h 42min to launch) demonstrates tactical responsiveness premium
key_concerns: $8B acquisition is 40% of RKLB's market cap — significant dilution risk
Pre-profitability on standalone basis — deal execution and integration risk are high
Still 20% below June highs; broader downtrend not yet reversed on technicals
fundamentals.summary: Grade C reflects Rocket Lab's pre-profitability stage and execution risks, partially offset by genuine strategic wins. The Iridium deal is transformational if integration succeeds, but it's also complex — operating a legacy LEO satellite network while scaling Electron launches is a multifaceted challenge.

risks: Iridium deal dilution: $8B acquisition at 40% of market cap is a large equity commitment — the stock-component of the deal will pressure share price if arbitrageurs short
$99–$100 round number resistance: closing just below $99.04 high signals sellers at the century mark; needs clean break to continue
EMA50 at $111.72 and EMA200 at $119.20 are both significant overhead resistance — full trend reversal requires closing above both
Iridium integration risk: operating a global satellite constellation is operationally different from launch services — management bandwidth concerns
